sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, timestamp_col TIMESTAMP, list_col ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2021-01-01 00:00:00', [1, 2, 3]), (2, TIMESTAMP '2021-01-02 00:00:00', [4, 5]), (3, TIMESTAMP NULL, []);
SELECT ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col) - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE timestamp_col IS NOT NULL) AS result FROM test_table;
SELECT id, ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col) - 1 AS adjusted_length FROM test_table WHERE adjusted_length IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, list_col FROM test_table WHERE ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col) - ARRAY_LENGTH(NULL) = 1;
SELECT id, (ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col) + 1) * 10 AS length_multiple FROM test_table WHERE timestamp_col - INTERVAL 1 DAY < TIMESTAMP '2021-01-02 00:00:00';
SELECT AVG(ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col)) AS avg_length FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING avg_length > 0;
SELECT id, list_col FROM test_table WHERE ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col) > (SELECT ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1);
SELECT id, (ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col) + 5) - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table) AS final_calculation FROM test_table WHERE timestamp_col IS NOT NULL;

SELECT id, CASE WHEN ARRAY_LENGTH(list_col) = 0 THEN 'empty' ELSE 'non-empty' END AS list_status FROM test_table WHERE timestamp_col - INTERVAL (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table) DAY < TIMESTAMP '2021-01-01 00:00:00';
